entry.title: How to use Document Information Extraction with CAPM (Node.js)?
Re: how to add a field in ALV of transaction IW47
entry.content: Okay but do you now stop at BREAK-POINT? If no, then did you check the Enhancement Spot? You have said "yes the enhancement is active" but it seems you were talking about your own Enhancement Implementation. I suggest that you learn the concept and elements of the Enhancement Framework, because you seem to be confused.

entry.title: Re: 🟣 Develop with SAP Build Apps Like a Pro

entry.content: Are you using DMS or Attachment service with your S/4HANA system? What is the method to upload files to S/4HANA, regardless of the frontend?

entry.title: Re: 🟣 Develop with SAP Build Apps Like a Pro
entry.content:
Thanks for your question. I'm not sure I fully understand what you are asking. Which technologies are you referring to? How do you want to get involved?

You can always start with a BTP trial system, https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/hcp-create-trial-account.html, and then install SAP Build Apps, https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ZpQM2B1v2GY

entry.title: Re: how to add a field in ALV of transaction IW47
entry.content:
Conclusion: I did the same code as you, i.e. extending OBJECT_TAB via the enhancement spot, adding the new field to the field catalog (CMOD IWOC0004 exit_saplirep1_001 ZXCSVU09), emptied the ALV field catalog buffer (BALVBUFDEL) because IW47/RIAFRU20 uses the ALV buffer, I ran the program, filled by debug the new field in OBJECT_TAB, and this value was displayed by the ALV of IW47.

Sorry, I can't do more.

NB: RIAUFR20 calls the FM REUSE_ALV_FIELDCATALOG_MERGE with the parameter I_INTERNAL_TABNAME="OBJECT_TAB" to determine the initial field catalog, this FM considers all custom fields appended to the included structures (RIHAFVR) but not the fields appended by the Enhancement Framework, hence you need to do an enhancement to update the field catalog for these fields.

NB: exit_saplirep1_001 documentation says "You can modify the field attributes in the field catalog IT_FIELDCAT using this customer exit. However, we must point out that the table as a whole should not be modified, that is, you should neither add nor delete rows."

Punith Oswal

entry.title: Re: SAP Build version not the same as tutorials.
entry.content:
Thanks everyone for writing about this issue and change. I am also working to understand what is the plan for REST APIs -- the (not really) new universal REST API interface of the old one as it appears in the tutorial. As soon as I find out if they will revert this back or keep it I will let you know. I am aware that the new REST API interface does no let me create what used to be called "URL placeholders" or custom fields and then add a value in the Test tab. 

Long story short, I will check what is happening and update the tutorials as needed.

I would also give you the current instructions here but I think there is a bug that prevents seeing the ID field in the Get record flow function. I will get back o this thread ASAP.

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Petoberfest 2024
Introduction to Universal Revenue Recognition
entry.content:
Universal Revenue Recognition?

What?

Is this a new solution from SAP for recognizing revenue?

To alleviate any excitement or confusion, it is not entirely new. Instead, it combines the already powerful capabilities of event-based revenue recognition and revenue accounting (also known as contract-based recognition) into a unified revenue recognition capability. As we have numerous revenue recognition customers with diverse needs, the main concept behind universal revenue recognition is to offer a comprehensive solution for all revenue recognition needs in one place.

Let me explain.

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, we offer a revenue recognition platform that comes with pre-delivered content and best practices (scope items) for various end-to-end scenarios and industries, such as projects, sales, services, and subscriptions.

For those who are familiar with the evolution of S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, it's worth noting that we have been supporting different turn-key scenarios with event-based revenue recognition (EBRR) from the very beginning. EBRR is utilized by industries that prioritize controlling and profitability reporting. It facilitates real-time calculation and posting of revenue and cost adjustments for sales orders, projects, service documents, and provider contracts, enabling real-time matching of costs and revenue. EBRR also includes market segment information for each revenue recognition posting and requires no reconciliation efforts between controlling and finance data since the information is stored in the Universal Journal. Therefore, EBRR is not only essential for revenue recognition but also a prerequisite for margin analysis and the evolution of Universal Parallel Accounting (UPA).

On the other hand, Revenue Accounting has historically been centered on IFRS 15 / ASC 606 external financial reporting and compliance requirements. It specifically caters to industries such as High-Tech and Telco, providing an accounting perspective on customer contracts that differs from the operational view, with a sophisticated revenue accounting contract management system. In S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, its usage has been restricted to BRIM related scenarios, and as a result, it is positioned as Contract-Based Revenue Recognition (CBRR) rather than Revenue Accounting.

Therefore, our approach is to tailor our solutions to individual use cases in various market segments, rather than applying one complex solution to every scenario. Our goal is to make revenue recognition as simple as possible, while still ensuring it meets all necessary requirements.

We believe this is essential because the needs in sales, project, or subscription scenarios can vary significantly. Professional services, for example, are driven by cost and profitability, where the flexibility in resource-related project billing and the percentage-of-completion (PoC) based on plan data plays a crucial role. On the other hand, for product sales in discrete industries, a strong integration into logistics is necessary, and customers require a correlation between cost and revenue for their revenue reporting. In the high-tech industry, we encounter even greater complexity with IFRS 15 due to contract-based revenue streams and frequent contract changes resulting from upsell or cross-sell scenarios.

Figure 1: Revenue Recognition in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

We also understand that streamlining revenue management and adapting to ever-changing business models are key factors for the success of every company.

Surely, companies utilize revenue recognition solutions to enable and automate compliance.

However, revenue recognition is more than just a legal compliance topic. In addition to the major regulatory changes in recent years from ASC 606 and IFRS 15, many companies are also facing radical disruptions in how customers purchase products and consume services. The traditional product business is now combined with adding services, or it is translated into a subscription business model. These independent sales and service transactions are increasingly being combined into agreements with multiple elements, allowing the company to offer holistic solutions. This puts additional focus on requirements around margin and profitability analysis.

How does Universal Revenue Recognition now come into play?

Universal Revenue Recognition combines SAP’s revenue recognition capabilities to offer a comprehensive solution for managing revenue. It aims to integrate the strengths of both accounting and controlling (EBRR) and IFRS15, as well as the contract management and allocation mechanisms of CBRR. This allows customers to choose the level of support for revenue recognition they need through configuration options. The following illustration shows the data flow for universal revenue recognition.

Figure 2: Overview Universal Revenue Recognition

Starting with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408, it is now possible to activate Universal Revenue Recognition (URR) for specific stock selling scenarios, service contracts, and fixed-price customer projects. If you choose to implement URR, you will need to consider scope items 3KK and 3VS for Contract-Based Revenue Recognition, in addition to the scope items for your respective integration scenario in Event-Based Revenue Recognition.

The URR functionality is optional and requires activation through expert configuration. This option was chosen to allow for a more controlled roll-out and to gather feedback. Once this initial step is completed, the solution can be configured according to your specific needs.

Revenue recognition rules are defined by EBRR revenue recognition keys, which outline the method for recognizing revenue and its posting rules. The key is derived from the item category and material from the operational document, such as a sales order or service contract. When using URR, you must flag the materials and item categories that participate in the URR process during configuration. This standard configuration is also used in the stand-alone EBRR scenario. If you utilize the URR process, you will also need to add relevant configuration for revenue accounting contract management.

Once that is completed, let's briefly review the process shown in figure 2. More detailed information will be provided in subsequent blogs for the specific scenarios.

Let's begin with a sales order in the Sales and Distribution module. In the sales order, you have entered one or more items which, based on previous configuration, are activated for Universal Revenue Recognition. A corresponding revenue recognition key has been configured and, as you are aware from the EBRR integration, derived from the configuration and stored in the sales order item. Additionally, for the sales order integration, you have defined a standalone selling price (SSP) in the SD pricing configuration through condition type PSAM (Step 1).

If the integration with URR has been properly set up, the integration component is triggered and creates a revenue contract and performance obligations. If you have experience with Revenue Accounting, this step is conducted through Inbound Processing – in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, we use the Optimized Inbound Processing. For the revenue contract, the revenue is allocated according to the SSP (Step 2).

Subsequently, the follow-on processes are initiated. For a sales order, this involves the delivery and goods issue, with the cost posting. From the cost posting, revenue is recognized in real-time, similar to EBRR, with the exception that the revenue to be recognized is taken from the allocated revenue in the associated revenue contract. Partial deliveries are also supported. Following EBRR posting rules, revenue is debited to an accrued revenue account against revenue adjustment. In the sales order process, the next step is billing for the delivery. Once again, using the logic from EBRR, the billed revenue is deferred in real-time, debiting the revenue adjustment account against deferred revenue (Step 3).

Revenue recognition is event-driven wherever events are available. However, there is also the regular period-end close. In the case of the sales order integration this triggers the balancing of the balance sheet accounts. For other scenarios, where revenue is recognized over time, revenue is recognized as earned for the financial period. This is the case for a service contract.

In the scenario of Universal Revenue Recognition, a new period-end close application job has been introduced, Run Revenue Recognition – Revenue Contracts, that performs the period end close for revenue contracts and its underlying operational documents, such as sales order, service contract and customer projects (Step 4). As a result, the usual period end close programs you may know from revenue accounting are not required anymore.

All posting are directly posting into the Universal Journal, with revenue contract and performance obligation as attributes in the posting lines (Step 5).

If you're interested, stay tuned for further detailed information on the individual scenarios and how universal revenue recognition will create this ‘one-stop shopping’ revenue recognition experience. This is just the beginning and URR capabilities will be growing steadily over time.

Additional Information from our user assistance you can find here:

Event-Based Revenue Recognition: SAP Help EBRR

Contract-Based Revenue Recognition: SAP Help CBRR

Universal Revenue Recognition:   SAP Help URR

entry.title: Re: SAP Business ByDesign - State Wise GST Configuration
entry.content:
Dear Vijay Kannan,

You have to create the separate supplier/customer master for each GST registration number of the single supplier/customer, with different supplying and billing addresses as per need and business requirement.

Best Regards,

Prathibha

entry.title: Re: SAP Build App data connection with Odata Integration.
entry.content:
As I always do with a bug, I try to check every part of he connection. That is difficult with the table, so first I do it with data variable.

  • Can you see data from your data resource in the data table?
  • If you create a data variable and connect to text component, can you see data?
  • If all that is fine, back on the table, can you check the configuraion that you are outputting fields that are not null?
  • Are you returning an enormous  data set that it is too big for SAP Build Apps? You can use the pagination features inside the table.

It is tough to diagnose the issue without knowing more and doing tests along the way.

All in all, my personal opinion is that the table component is not the best to use, and it is better o make your own table component using a data variable.

entry.title: SOURCING & PROCUREMENT - PURCHASE RATE CONTRACT IN SAP CLOUD
entry.content:
Dear Team

Basically our clients requirement is RATE CONTRACT but in SAP we have only quantity contract & value contract. We want when We  create po with reference to  value contract , price & condition should copy in po from value contract and it should not be editable in PO. user will only enter quantity in po.  Also we are not able to add condition in value contract like basic price , discount etc. Please let us know whether schema can assign to Contract or we have to add condition through condition record.   

 

Rajesh

entry.title: The Love-Hate Relationship with Networking
entry.content:
Networking. Probably one of the most polarizing soft skills in the business world. A real love-hate relationship you could say. It comes natural to some and can be energizing, while others just wish they would never have to do it because it drains their social batteries and increases their anxiety. Many swear by it and encourage to use every opportunity we get to grow our network. But do we really need it to have a successful career?

To me the question here is more one about perspective. But let me share some of my insights on what I learned about networking from our recent Summer Summit collaboration between the Business Women’s Network and SAP Women in Tech. A few weeks ago, we organized our first ever joined Summer Summit on-site in Walldorf. A little bit late for a summer summit in September, some might say, but we had the sun gracing us with one of those last warm days this year. We were lucky enough to have Traci Hughes present us with a short keynote on the topic of (you probably guessed it): Networking. And while at first people were expecting some encouraging words to please start networking because it is career essential, Traci managed to find a slightly more nuanced perspective on the topic.

Let me sum up the most important things I learned from that evening. Being an outgoing person myself, I am of course not as aware of all the aspects. But even I have encountered events and situations where I just could not bring myself to talk to another stranger, or a semi-acquaintance and discuss the current weather conditions because it felt incredibly draining.

The bad news first: networking really can be helpful in your professional development.

However, it might be a relief for some that when it comes to networking, quality can weigh a lot more than quantity. Which means fostering or working on good connections can be more helpful than just having plenty without really knowing someone.

Something I also learned about networking is that it kind of works like a garden. Meaning it grows over time and harvesting the fruits of your networking can take quite a while. Personally, I still feel like I am waiting for that bountiful harvest 😉 but you never know, right?

Lastly, I think it is important to control that pressure we are putting on ourselves to constantly perform our best social skills or come up with the most entertaining anecdote and topic to talk to someone. In a nutshell: being someone we are not. And if you benefit from good quality networks that are none superficial, doesn’t trying to be someone else counterfeit that purpose? Meaning, be yourself. And if that means you won’t talk to 10 new people at every given opportunity, I think that’s absolutely justified behavior.

Because the really good conversations that help you in your professional development are those that feel authentically you. And let me tell you: those conversations, we have had plenty of at our Summer Event. 😊

At long last I want to encourage all readers to look for those authentic conversations that will grow your network. And yes, you might have to nudge yourself a little bit outside of your comfort zone at times. Yes, there will certainly be those awkward moments around new people, but I don’t think anyone has ever grown without being a little uncomfortable throughout the process. Do you?

P.S. Feel free to share some of your awkward networking stories with the community!!

entry.title: Re: Please explain Conditional router in detail in CPI
entry.content:
Hi Sriprasad,

 

 

I am using looping process call with condition.

${property.value1} != 'one' or ${property.value2} != 'two'.

in locall intergration process i am setting content modifier with value1 as two and value2 as two. 

so second time it should not come to local intergration process. but it is iterating again.

 

Please let me know if the syntax is incorrect and how can i correct it. It will be a great help

entry.title: Re: Identity and Access Management with Microsoft Entra, Part I: Managing access to SAP BTP
entry.content:
Hi @tskwin ,

I checked my settings im IAS and they are as follows:

Application Settings of the BTP subaccount:

  • Subject name identifier: Identity Directory, Email
  • Attributes: email, email_verified, family_name, given_name, user_uuid -> all sourced from Identity Directory, only groups comes from the corporate IDP as described in the blog

Corporate Identity Provider:

  • Subject name identifier: None (sub claim is the identifier)

I created a fresh BTP trial account and IAS trial tenant for this tutorial and did not change any of the default settings for the subject name identifiers and attributes. Only the groups attribute is added as described.

You may also want to go this path, or  change you settings accordingly. Let us know if it works.

Best regards

Martin

entry.title: Re: Volume /hana/log is full with log segments in state F...
entry.content:
You can safely release log segments anytime using the SQL statement below. There is no need to stop the application. (You must not use OS commands, like rm, to remove log segments. This would make your instance unusable.). 
The SQL will just remove log segments in status FREE, which are neither required for crash recovery nor any other purpose (HSR, to be backed up, etc.). 
The only drawback is, that HANA has to format new log segments next time there are no log segments in status FREE. Usually, formatting log segments is not an issue unless you suffer from slow disk I/O.
On the other hand there is no need to remove any log segments unless you want to free the disk space. Keep in mind that XFS file system does not support reducing the file system size at all, even not offline (just in case you use XFS). 
Regards, 
  Alexander

entry.title: Using RAP Generator, Put Operation fails
entry.content:
I am using the RAP Generator Wizard in Eclipse ("Generate ABAP Repository Objects ...") on a behaviour definition of a standard SAP RAP object.

Like in the RAP630 tutorial I use the behaviour definition I_BANKTP and try to create an "OData UI Service".

In the BTP ABAP Trial Account everything works fine, but in our SAP S/4 HANA 2023 private Cloud system the wizard tries to create all objects but ends with the error "PUT operation failed".

Does anyone else has this error? Do you know how to sove this problem?

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Techtoberfest 2024
entry.content: Nothing special, just 2 external monitors 🙂

entry.title: The Rise of AI Agents: Transforming Business and Beyond
entry.content:
AI Agents

As someone deeply interested in the field of artificial intelligence, I’ve had the privilege of observing its remarkable evolution firsthand. Today, I want to share some insights into how AI agents are not only reshaping the business landscape but are also poised to transform the way we live and work.

 

Open AI 5 Levels to Reach AGI

The AI Revolution: More Than Just Hype

We are in the midst of a true AI revolution. What started with simple chatbots has rapidly evolved into highly sophisticated systems that touch almost every aspect of our daily lives and work environments. In just the past few weeks, we’ve seen groundbreaking advancements, particularly in tools developed by leading AI companies like OpenAI. These innovations are pushing the boundaries of what AI can do—creating more intelligent, reliable, and efficient systems that are becoming essential to modern businesses.

Enter AI Agents: The Next Frontier in Automation

One of the most exciting developments in AI is the rise of AI agents. Unlike traditional chatbots that are limited to scripted conversations, AI agents are complex systems capable of reasoning, learning, and autonomously executing tasks with minimal human input. These agents can communicate naturally, connect to external applications (like CRM systems or email platforms), and even make decisions based on real-time data from the company. Source

What does this mean for businesses? AI agents can streamline operations, improve customer interactions, and transform problem-solving processes across industries. The shift from chatbots to AI agents marks a significant leap forward—one that many industry experts believe will redefine the workforce. In fact, some predict that by 2025, there could be more AI agents operating in the workplace than human employees. Source

How to Work with AI Agents: A Human-Centered Approach

As AI continues to advance, it’s important to approach it with the right mindset. While AI agents can handle increasingly complex tasks, the human element remains critical to ensure these systems function optimally and ethically. Here’s a practical framework for working with AI:

 

  • Always include AI in decision-making processes: AI agents can offer insights that might not be immediately obvious to humans. Bringing them into the conversation early allows businesses to leverage their full potential.
  • Be the human in the loop: Even with highly autonomous AI agents, human oversight is essential. This ensures that systems are aligned with business goals and can adapt to changes in real-time.
  • Treat AI as a collaborator, not just a tool: The concept of anthropomorphism—treating AI as a 'partner' rather than just software—helps foster better interaction and integration within teams.
  • Stay adaptable: AI technology is advancing at a breakneck pace. What is cutting-edge today could be outdated tomorrow. Businesses must remain flexible, continually adopting new tools and approaches to stay competitive.

 

Source

What Sets AI Agents Apart from Traditional Chatbots?

It’s essential to understand what makes AI agents different from the chatbots we’ve all grown accustomed to. Traditional chatbots typically operate within set rules and scripts, handling basic customer service queries or directing users to the right resources.

AI agents, however, are much more powerful. Here are some key features that distinguish AI agents from chatbots:

 

  • Powered by advanced natural language processing (NLP): AI agents can understand and respond in more human-like ways, making interactions smoother and more intuitive.
  • Connected to outside applications: They don’t just engage in conversations—they can connect with external systems like CRM, supply chain management, and email platforms to perform tasks.
  • Reasoning and planning: AI agents can analyze situations and develop plans based on real-time data, allowing them to make informed decisions autonomously.
  • Access to company data: They can be fine-tuned using company-specific data, making their outputs more relevant and effective.
  • Autonomous execution of tasks: Beyond just offering suggestions, AI agents can execute tasks semi-autonomously, such as processing orders or responding to customer queries.
  • Continuous learning and improvement: These systems are designed to learn from their interactions and continuously improve over time.

 

Source

The Road Ahead: AI’s Role in the Future of Work

The future of AI is incredibly bright, with applications that will continue to disrupt traditional business models. From improving customer experiences to automating complex workflows, AI agents are already reshaping industries. But this is just the beginning.

As AI agents become more capable, business leaders and professionals need to embrace these changes. Adopting AI tools and integrating them into core business functions isn’t just an option—it’s a necessity for staying competitive in a rapidly evolving marketplace.

One thing is clear: AI is no longer just a futuristic concept. It’s here now, transforming the way we live and work. By understanding its capabilities and learning how to work alongside AI agents, we can unlock new levels of innovation, efficiency, and success in our organizations.

Conclusion: How Will AI Agents Impact Your Industry?

The rise of AI agents presents exciting opportunities and challenges across industries. Whether you’re in finance, healthcare, retail, or any other sector, the impact of these systems will be profound.

What are your thoughts on the rise of AI agents? How do you see them changing your industry? I’d love to hear your perspectives—let’s continue the conversation in the comments.

#ArtificialIntelligence #BusinessInnovation #FutureOfWork #AIAgents #Automation #slithoughtleadership #sapbusinessAI

entry.title: Cannot delete untransported Business Configuration custom entry, please help

Somehow some forms were duplicated in the list. We tried to delete the not needed ones, but we cannot delete any new entries, despite, we have not transported any of these new entries and of course they were never used.

entry.title: Re: open attachment from webclient
entry.content: SAP Business One web client uploads attachment files through Service Layer. According to the user manual of Service Layer, for the Service Layer running on SAP HANA on Linux, it is not allowed to directly access the attachment folder. Please refer to SAP Note 2312947

entry.title: Re: Need help with HL segment mapping for EDI ASN 856
entry.content: I have a similar requirement. Were you able to achieve it? If so, how did you do it? Thanks, Shafi

entry.title: Re: Need Help in populating EDI 856 HL segments in correct order !
entry.content: I have a similar requirement. Were you able to achieve it? Thanks, Shafi

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Petoberfest 2024
Re: SAP BTP Identity Services logon against SAP ECC or S4 backend?
entry.content:
@tongzheng: Thank you for that concise and knowledge packed response. Given all these options, I think option #3 would deliver in simplicity and requirements. Not to mention, we don't have a JAVA server (Thankfully).  

So my next step is to figure out how to delivery:
(3) You can setup ECC system as the source of Identity Provisioning and replicate all ABAP users into Identity Directory of Cloud Identity Service. In this case the authentication will happen entirely on BTP but users can login using their ECC user credentials.

I don't need a step by step guide, but a high level description of what I need to do would be greatly appreciated.  I believe the replication is done via the Identity Provisioning Service, configure the target system as my ECC destination and map the fields?  I would really just appreciate a line of sight on the major steps needed and we can wrap this thread up.  Points will be awarded.  Thanks!

entry.title: Re: SapJCo3: SNC between Java application with multiple S...
entry.content:
For the steps regarding the configuration of the JCo DestinationProvider you would need to use the parameters jco.client.mshost, jco.client.msserv, jco.client.group & jco.client.r3name instead of jco.client.ashost and jco.client.sysnr.  Unable to say with certainty if that requires any changes to the PSE configuration, however.

 

Regards,

Ryan Crosby

entry.title: Re: Customer field Not shown in ME21n and ME22N
entry.content: Customer field Not shown in ME21n and ME22N

entry.title: Re: Customer field Not shown in ME21n and ME22N
entry.content: urgent req

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Petoberfest 2024
entry.content: It’s not all just work. Let’s have some fun!

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Petoberfest 2024
entry.content:
Meet Coco, the watchful guardian of my workspace! Whether I'm coding or brainstorming, he's always there to supervise and keep things running smoothly. Nothing gets past his sharp eyes!

#Petoberfest #DevBuddy #CodingWithCoco #devtoberfest

entry.title: Problems with the new SAP build service
entry.content:
I don't know where to get help, because it seems that SAP has no direct support, at least not for Appgyver users! So I hope to get some hints on how to proceed here: I managed to upload almost the whole Appgyver android package (app id 106287, version 3.0.1) to the renewed SAP build service until it stopped with an error.                                                                                                                                                                     I didn't have any problems with the old build service I used in July 2024.
I decided to make a second try by choosing the option "new configuration" - reloaded the necessary certificates I used before - also chose "custom assets", gave the passwords etc.
but the build process still stopped in error after about 80%.
Attached you find the generated report files of both tries (looks like identical).
Please advice me. What is wrong? How should I proceed?

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud, embedded edition – the Way Ahead
entry.content:
@SaikrishnaBollineni,

The main use case for SAC Embedded Edition was for embedding analytics into partner-built BTP solutions.  Can you clarify why this service is being retired?

entry.title: Re: Hi, I am unable to find onboarding practice system in...
entry.content: Hi Margit, I presume that there is no hands on practice system for RMK? I see one for RCM but no luck finding an RMK practice

entry.title: Re: Your own S/4HANA sandbox at home?
entry.content:
The new free version of SAP for developers can still be installed on a $120 MiniPC. Admittedly, I had to expand the memory a bit this time, but it is still possible. In the video I describe how I migrated my sandbox based on ABAP Platform Trial 1909 to the latest version of the free trial system from SAP. ABAP Cloud Developer Trial 2022, contrary to its name, can still be run on a local computer. I invite you to watch a video about it.

If anyone is interested, I invite you to watch the video.

entry.title: Query on certification
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

Please help me out on this. I am planning to schedule my SAP certification exam in mid October. While scheduling the exam I will be in India but while taking the exam I will be in another country. Will my location allow me to take exam or I need to take the exam in India only.

Kindly help.

Best Regards

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Petoberfest 2024
entry.content: Cookie the SAP Cat

entry.title: Re: Memory related runtime errors in SAP
entry.content: Heap memory is carried from Disk storage or the RAM?

entry.title: Financial Document (Letter of Credit) FTR_CREATE further processing in SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud
entry.content:
Hello Experts, 

Could you please advise on the steps to be followed for Letter of Credit (L/c) Financial Document processing in SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud. 

we had created Financial document using t-code FTR_CREATE and assigned in Sales Order. However we could not further enable settings/configuration regarding Financial Document (L/c) validity check and value check in further subsequent process. 

Also please advise on how the below configuration is done in SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud. 

Please advise. 

Thank you,

kind regards

Thangaraj Thomas

entry.title: Error in creation material master MM01
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I created a material master by MM01 and the material XXXX was created successfully message was also displayed. The material is created but not updated in any material master table such as MARA, MARD etc. and no record of that material in that plant and location. After successfully creation of material  error " Express document update was cancelled recieved from the author XXXXX" was poped up. Then I created the material with basic tabs only and extended the material to all views one by one and I found thta the error is due to some configration mismatching in Accounting1 view. Even though my material ledger is active but unabale to save the material due to Accounting1 view. Please guide me to solve the issues.

entry.content: Meet Snowy aka Lady White, my (friend's) new pet 🙂

It is worth to mention here that above what comments are applicable if it's about DIA WP.

entry.title: Re: 🔵 Getting Started with Agents using SAP Generative AI Hub

entry.title: How can I keep a SU number when moving a SU from storage type to another storage type?
entry.content: How can I keep a SU number when moving a SU from storage type to another storage type based on a TR? Both storage types a SU managed. SAP generates as per standard a new SU number when I move a SU from storage type 1 to storage type 2 creating a TO from a TR. Yes it works ok with tx LT10 but not when creating a TR from a TR.

entry.title: Re: How to use Document Information Extraction with CAPM...
entry.content:
I think you could have explained any particular issue around this topic.

Here is a custom plugin - CAP-DOX- to extract the PDFs using the Document Information Extraction service. Visit - Git repo for code base and usage. And here is the SAP blog -  https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/sap-ai-services-document-information-extraction-service-gen-ai-with-cap/ba-p/13718571

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Techtoberfest 2024
entry.content:
Hello everyone!

I wanted to share with you a picture of my humble setup. It's not the most extravagant, but it's where the magic of coding happens. 🤓

In my space, I use custom GPTs that are like having a team of virtual assistants ready to help me with development. Sometimes I feel like the keyboard types by itself! 🧞‍♂️

Of the VS Code tools, Prettier is the one I use the most, keeping my code more organized than my own desk (which isn't saying much 🙈).

Greetings from Bolivia!

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Petoberfest 2024
entry.content: I only have some socks with bees: bees => honey => honey mead 😁

entry.title: BNJ and BLD test scripts are not supported in test automation tool
entry.content:
Hi,

In test automation tool when I want to select master data topics to create a test process, they are not displayed to me.

I got the master data scopes from below links.

https://support.sap.com/content/dam/SAAP/Sol_Pack/BP_CLD_ENTPR/BP_CLD_ENTPR_S4CLD2202_27_Master_Data_EN_XX.htm

https://help.sap.com/docs/s4hana-best-practices/s4cld2408-master-data/start

Any idea?

BR,

Amin

entry.title: Re: cl_http_destination_provider=>create_by_cloud_destination erroring
Display a List and Allow Form Printing in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I am new to developing applications in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and I am seeking guidance on how to create an app in the public version. My goal is to develop an application that:

  • Displays a list of products based on specific logic.
  • Includes a button that allows printing a form for those products.

Could you help me with advice or resources on how to get started with this? Any guide, tutorial, or example would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you very much in advance for your support!

entry.title: Re: HANA Express Docker on Ubuntu
entry.content:
With the help of @UweFetzer_se38  discovered that adding:

-h b6557dacdfc6
in front of 
 

 

--name hxe

 

fixes the issue and now the HXE is also running on my Ubuntu 22.04.5 LTS.

entry.title: Re: Introducing the new module of SAP SuccessFactors in 2H2022 – Dynamic Teams
entry.content:
@PrakharV

Story Reports is the only SF reporting tool supporting Dynamic Teams. Please review this video with an example report presented at exactly 4min of the video.

Dynamic Teams: Optimizing Outcomes for Cross-Functional Teams - https://www.sap.com/assetdetail/2023/06/5854ebbf-797e-0010-bca6-c68f7e60039b.html

Regards, Piotr

entry.title: fiscal representative concept-Intrastat
entry.content:
One small help, I think this could not be possible in the sap standard solution, but could you please let me know how to do in SAP 

Ex: 1000 Company code (CH) –1004 plant (BE Plant ).
To main Entity 1000 company code BE plant is assigned.
So, we can send the Intrastat declarations based on 1000 Company code and 1004 Plant.

No issue, in the above case.
Now, we got the plan to implement for some other countries like FR, IT, PL,DE,SI, ES.
Above countries have different company IDs with different plants.
Now , in the above countries don’t have specific registered plants (only virtual plants not real plants), so businesses is using the fiscal representative concept.
While sending dispatches, the company code should always be 1000 ( CH ).

In this case, how we can send the declarations for the countries FR, IT, PL,DE,SI, ES based on fiscal representative?

entry.title: Re: Transitive attribute in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hello,

Please specify that this works only with analytic models (SAC front end and BO 4.3 sp4 with an OLAP connection), even if during modeling of view you can specify associations.

Every other BI tool that access to Datasphere's database through ODBC can not see analytic models (missing privileges to read column views in the main schema of a space)

and so by transitive logic law it third party BI tools can not see transitive attributes.

apart from this, a question to SAP:

why do I need to specify a fixed list of attributes in Analytic Model? why by default isn't every attribute selectable from Front end tool?
if I add an attribute potentially I need to fix manually a hundred of analytic models to expose it: this is a big problem for 2 reasons:

- a level of maintenance even bigger than what BW Query does.

- the list of attribute is in the back end, while reports are in front end tools (SAC). So this increase the maintenance as there are usually 2 different developer involved in a simple change (chat, email, meeting and so on).

From this and recent updates on transport and validation of packages,
it seems to me that imposing strict consistency checks lead to increase the amount of developer work and the complexity of your software (the number of bug to be fixed)!!!
Recently for example I am not able to export / import objects manually, the import through CSN file lose its functionality of being able to select exactly which objects to import (probably due to this constant push for developing locks and mechanism to guarantee correctness that needs to be in charge of the person in front of the computer).

As a result to export / import a single object I need :

1) pass through a package (either object package (with other locks on searching for correctness of dependencies => another problem), or normal old packages => 10 times more click than the standard Export / Import).

2) or delete manually from the CSN .JSON file the objects that I do not want to import. (the much preferred choice for me).

namaste

entry.title: Bulk PDF Generation [thousands of unique customer statements every month]
entry.content:
Has anybody figured out how to quickly/efficiently generate thousands of unique 'customer statements' every month [or something similar] using some sort of scalable method [besides looping on a single machine or two] - like an Azure Web Service that can scale up with demand?

Generating PDFs 'serially' takes forever and uses a lot of resources.  We use the exact same RPT file, but have to programmatically run it once for each customer in our database [changing the customer id in the parameter].  Is there a better way, I mean doing the looping works but pegs the CPU and takes forever.

 I would want to do it in Azure, but I don't think the Crystal dlls can be referenced from an Azure web service, can it?  Can referencing the runtime dlls and adding them to the bin folder of the web service - on a machine where the runtime installer has not been run, work?  Has anybody done that?  It would let me load balance and scale it up.

entry.title: Re: SapJCo3: SNC between Java application with multiple S...
entry.content:
Hi Ryan. Many thanks for the reply. There are 2 kinds of connection I can make to the SAP systems: a direction connection (using the SAP ABAP server host name and instance number) and a logon-balancing connection. For a start I am using direct connections, in which I am setting, among other parameters, jco.client.sysnr and jco.client.ashost. The SNC connections work as I explained so I don't think this is the issue. Your suggestion is important for logon-balancing connections.

Further details about my situation: Lets take the simpler scenario for SNC connection where no certificate from the Java app needs to be imported into the SAP system. We just need the PSE from latter to create the cred_v2 file on the side of the Java app. On the server where the Java application runs, SECUDIR environment variable has been defined. This is where the PSE and cred_v2 files will be located. The Java app is run by an operating system user say "foo". The cred_v2 file is created from the command

 

sapgenpse seclogin -p pse_file_name -O foo

 

Running this the first time for a SAP system creates the cred_v2 file. For the second SAP system, running same command with the right pse_file and user, I can see from the contents of the cred_v2 file there are now credentials for reading the 2 different PSE files. Now I test connection. The first SAP system I get a connection successful and can do more with the connection. For the second however, I get error: GSS-API(maj): Miscellaneous failure GSS-API(min): A2210223:Server does not trust my certificate path target="..." Drilling down to see the logs on the second SAP system. I notice that the certificate infos of the first SAP system is sent during the connection attempt, which ought not to be. For simplicity, I remove time stamps as every line represent an action in some time. I have checked to make sure from the Java app, the right connection details for the second are sent but it appears deep during the connection only the first entry in the cred_v2 file is always used.

 

Certificate verification result (failed)
 ----- BEGIN VERIFICATION RESULT -----
# --- Messages -----------
INFO: Verification time - Sun Sep 29 18:00:55 2024
ERROR: The verified certificate chain is complete but no certificate is trusted.
# --- Summary -----------
#01 Certificate (End Entity): VALID
Subject:                      CN=SAPSYSTEM1, O=Company, C=DE
Issuer:                       CN=SAPSYSTEM1, O=Company, C=DE
Fingerprint (SHA256):         6A:60:2B:7B:1B:82:46:81:25:9F:D0:9D:CF:73:C2:62:5A:D5:25:4B:DD:35:30:30:6F:36:54:BC:7F:BF:80:CD
Validity:                     Wed Sep 25 13:00:01 2024 / Fri Jan  1 01:00:01 2038
PKI validation:               FAILED
Trusted:                      ERROR: Untrusted - Complete Chain
# --- Results -----------
Certificate Result #01:       FAILED
Certificate (End Entity):     [6A:60:2B:7B] CN=SAPSYSTEM1, O=Company, C=DE
Trusted:                      ERROR: Untrusted - Complete Chain
Policy:                       Untested
Revocation:                   Untested
OCSP:                         Untested
Issuer:                       Untested
CRLs:                         Untested
# --- Environment -----------
Trust in PSE:
Token URI                      : tokpse:/usr/sap/SAPSYSTEM2/D00/sec/SAPSNCS.pse
Trusted certificate            : CN=SAPSYSTEM2, O=Company, C=DE
Trusted certificate            : CN=SAPSYSTEM2, O=Company, C=DE
----- END VERIFICATION RESULT -----
Srv-80000000: Peer certificate not trusted

 

This is the output from the "sapgenpse seclogin -l" command

 

PS C:\Path\to\secudir> sapgenpse seclogin -l
 running seclogin with USER="foo"

 0 (LPS:OFF): CN=SAPSYSTEM1, O=Company, C=DE
         (LPS:OFF): C:\Path\to\secudir\sap-system-1.pse

 1 (LPS:OFF): CN=SAPSYSTEM2, O=Company, C=DE
         (LPS:OFF): C:\Path\to\secudir\sap-system-2.pse

 2 readable SSO-Credentials available

entry.title: TM FSD PROCESSING
entry.content: Hello, fsd stuck at status: FRS initialization started, how do I reprocess

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
My name is Nguyễn Thế Võ Lâm, and I am a recent graduate working as an SAP SD consultant. I have always had a passion for business and aspire to improve my skills while expanding my knowledge of SAP. I believe this opportunity would be a great starting point for me to learn and grow in the field of SAP solution design. I also strongly resonate with the desire to understand how businesses operate and optimize processes to deliver better products and services to their customers.

I believe that this digital skills initiative will not only help me improve myself but also enable me to share my knowledge and experience with others in the future, contributing to helping those who wish to learn and grow, just like me.

entry.title: Re: Migrate the ASE database (On-Prem) to the SAP hana Cl...
entry.content:
Hi Javier,
Thank you for the response.
Now I am successfully able to connect with the database with the same IP for gateway as well as ASE database.

It was an issue with the Password Encyption at the server end. Once we have disabled that , we are able to access the ASE database with the same method described in the blog. However , it gives another error related to non-SAP database.

"SAP Advanced SQL Migration cannot be used to access an SAP ERP database.
Database 'XXX' looks like an SAP ERP database.
Quitting...
Disconnected (via gateway) from DBMS server."

Does it mean that this SAP ASMT tool does not allow to access the ASE database with the SAP ERP? Is this the case even with latest version of the SAP Advanced SQL migration tool(we are using version 3.5.3)?

As per my understand from Feb 2024 blog updates, SAP ASE is also allowed as a source database. It does not specify that it should not be connected to SAP ERP database.Please confirm.

If this is not possible via extract, will extract_offline will be supported with the DDL scripts from ASE database with SAP ERP?

If it is not possible even with offline_extract method, what shall be the best way forward? Could you please advise?

We have made lot of efforts to enable the gateway , SAP ASMT configuration to connect to the host server database and now we are stuck. So,please provide your advice here.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello,

I’m very interested in participating in the digital skills initiative. I hold a Bachelor’s degree in Management Information Systems and possess strong soft skills that will help me succeed as a SAP Consultant. I’m particularly interested in the Financial Accounting module and eager to deepen my knowledge of SAP to advance my career.

Thank you for considering my application!

Best,
Hassan Ezzat

entry.title: Re: How to work with test cases and test plans in several...
entry.content:
Hello Colleagues, 

Test Plan copy feature is planned, you can copy content from one test plan to another. 

Test cases are created for a project and you can reuse the test cases created within the project.

Thanks,

Aj

entry.title: Re: Add master data scope items to Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Hello Colleagues,

You can upload test cases aswell which are in the excel format, however mass upload is planned. 

Thanks,

Aj

entry.title: Custom Extension for Worklist
entry.content:
Hi all,

I have been trying to create a custom plugin that will allow your users to filter the worklist within an Operation Activity Pod.

I have created a custom plugin that consists of the operationPodSelectionExensionProvider and the workListExtensionProvider.

I have successfully added an input field

What I cannot work out is however in the worklist I can use this to modify the worklist table data to only return the SFCs where my input matches the Sales Order column.

As an alternative approach I was going to do a seprate AJAX call ontop of the existing predefined worklist call to get a list of orders and then pass these into into the updateSearchFilter function but it appears this is limited to only allow single values and not multiple like I need?

Am I going about this in the correct way or should I be instead be building a view plugin for my new filter to be passed in and essentially recreate the worklist as custom pod view plugin?

Any suggestions?
Thanks
Ben

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hi @David_Chaviano 

My name is Mustafa Moin, and I am currently work as an educational consultant in the special education field. I've always had a strong passion for business, but I've often struggled to connect this interest with my educational background. However, I see this opportunity as the perfect steppingstone toward a new career as an SAP solution designer. Your analogy about the amusement park experience resonated with me, and I can relate to wanting a deeper understanding of how businesses function, optimize processes, and deliver exceptional products and services to their customers.

I believe this digital skills initiative is not only a fantastic way to develop personally but also a chance to contribute meaningfully by helping others take their first step toward transforming their lives. While I can guarantee my continued motivation to learn, I find it even more important to commit to paying it forward by sharing my knowledge and experiences with others when the time comes.

Thank you for this incredible opportunity.

Mustafa Moin

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Petoberfest 2024
entry.content: Me and my backend team

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Petoberfest 2024
Re: Devtoberfest 2024 Week 1 Contest Activities
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I’m currently working through the tutorial "Create a CAP Business Service with Node.js Using Visual Studio Code" (found here: SAP Tutorial) and I've hit a snag at STEP 7 - Add Initial Data.

After executing the command "cds add data", I expected the database tables to be created with initial data, and CSV files with a header line for all entities to be added to the db/data folder. However, nothing seems to have been generated in that directory.

I’ve submitted an issue on GitHub detailing my situation: GitHub Issue.

It seems like there might be some missing steps, or perhaps I’ve overlooked something. If anyone has successfully completed this step, could you please provide some guidance on what might be going wrong? I’ve also attached screenshots to illustrate the issue.

Thanks in advance for your help!

Best,

Francisco.

entry.title: SAP SLICENSE - Warning Message while Login after we renewed it
entry.content:
Hi SAP,

In GGP Production system, we have renewed our SLICENSE for our corresponding Hardware key.

Even though, we are getting the warning pop message while login to the system.

Could you please help to fix this issue.

Regards,

Krishna

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Petoberfest 2024

entry.title: Re: Devtoberfest 2024 Week 2 Contest Activities
entry.content:
Feedback on "Create an ABAP Core Data Services (CDS) View in SAP BTP, ABAP Environment" Tutorial

Hello everyone,

I wanted to give a quick heads up to those working on the tutorial “Create an ABAP Core Data Services (CDS) View in SAP BTP, ABAP Environment”. I encountered a small issue with the code in STEP 9 and made some adjustments to resolve it.

I've provided my feedback and proposed changes in this GitHub issue: Feedback on Step 9.

Feel free to check it out, and I hope it helps improve the tutorial for everyone!

Best regards,

Francisco Milan.

entry.title: Re: Define shipping data for Plants (STO)
entry.content: even I am facing the same issue, were you able to get the solution. if yes, then please share
